title: release: add plan --out and apply --from
summary: This PR cleanly refactors the planner store from a dir-centric to a path-centric API, adding `--out` and `--from` flags to `plan` and `apply` commands respectively. The design is sound: a single `resolvePlanLocation(path?)` function acts as the canonical resolution point, and the active-plan defaults are preserved as zero-argument calls. Effect laziness is correctly relied upon for module-level constants. Two minor issues found: redundant double-resolution in CLI commands, and a pre-existing README table formatting issue with pipe characters.

findings.title: Double path resolution in CLI commands
findings.body: In both `apply.ts` and `plan.ts`, `resolvePlanLocation(planPath)` is called explicitly to obtain `planLocation` for display purposes, and then immediately `read(planPath)` / `write(plan, planPath)` each call `resolvePlanLocation` internally a second time with the same input. This causes two redundant Effect evaluations doing identical work. Consider threading `planLocation` through to avoid the double resolution, e.g. by accepting an already-resolved `ActivePlanLocation` in the store functions, or by deriving the display string from the same `planLocation` returned by the inner call.

findings.title: README table pipe characters break Markdown rendering
findings.body: The `|` characters inside `<official | candidate | ephemeral>` are not escaped within a Markdown table cell, so most parsers will interpret them as column separators and break the table layout. This was a pre-existing issue that the PR extends. Fixing it requires either escaping (`\|`) or using a code-span that wraps the whole lifecycle argument so the pipes are rendered literally.

residual_risks: The resource-layer functions (`resource.read`, `resource.write`, `resource.delete`) now receive `Fs.Path.$Abs` (which can be either AbsFile or AbsDir) rather than always AbsDir. The correctness of this depends on `resolvePlanFile` being called inside those resource functions — this is visible in the existing `resource.ts` logic but the full `@kitz/resource` API surface was not reviewable since the files weren't in the current worktree.

No integration test covers the case where `--out` and `--from` are passed mismatched paths (e.g. plan written to A, apply reads from B). This is an expected user-error path but currently produces no specific error message.
